title: Gently Grilled Peruvian Beef Saltado
description: A FODMAP-friendly twist on the classic Peruvian dish, featuring tender beef, pillowy rice, and a tangy shoyu-koji sauce.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the beef to the skillet and cook for 3 minutes per side, or until browned and cooked to desired level of doneness.
Transfer the beef to a cutting board and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly.
In the same skillet, add the sliced red onion and cook for 3-4 minutes, or until softened.
Add the sliced bell pepper to the skillet and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until tender.
In a small bowl, whisk together the rice vinegar, soy sauce, shoyu-koji, ginger, garlic, sugar, salt, and black pepper.
Pour the sauce over the vegetables in the skillet and stir to combine.
Add the sliced beef back into the skillet and stir to coat with the sauce.
Serve the beef and vegetables over a bed of steamed rice, garnished with chopped green onions.
Cook the rice according to package instructions or use leftover cooked rice.
